summary: Dark Star jam and Corinna shine; solid Hornsby‑Jerry interplay
blurb: The show delivers a scorching Dark Star/Space excursion and a fiery Corinna medley, highlighted by tight Hornsby‑Garcia interplay. Jerry’s guitar is rich on the early jams while Phil lays down a phat bass line throughout.
review: On March 9, 1992 at the Capital Centre, the Dead rolled out a setlist that balanced classic roots with late‑era firepower. The first set opened with a tight “Jack Straw” and surged through “Cassidy > Deal,” setting a high‑energy tone that carried into an electric second half. The centerpiece was the dark‑starred voyage: a soaring Dark Star that melted into space, followed by a blistering “Corinna” jam where Jerry’s thoughtful lead wove perfectly with Bruce Hornsby’s piano textures. The post‑drums medley of “Victim Or The Crime > Iko Iko > Corinna” kept the momentum blazing, and the evening closed on a haunting “Morning Dew.” While Jerry showed signs of fatigue toward the end, his early‑night brilliance and the band’s tight rhythm section made this a standout 1992 performance. Fans looking for the definitive capture should gravitate to the crisp soundboard recording that showcases every nuance without dropouts.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings praise the show (Dark Star, Corinna, Hornsby‑Garcia interplay) while two are mixed and one negative; overall sentiment is clearly positive but not unanimous, warranting a moderate +1.2 boost.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, clean soundboard feed with excellent instrument separation; captures the full show without hiss or dropouts.

key_highlights: Scorching Dark Star > Space excursion with tight Hornsby‑Garcia interplay
Fiery Corinna medley and post‑drums Victim Or The Crime > Iko Iko > Corinna sequence
Strong early set featuring Jack Straw, Cassidy > Deal, and Row Jimmy

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a rich, thoughtful guitar tone on Dark Star and Corinna with standout interplay with Bruce Hornsby; energy tapers slightly later in the night.
band_performance.Phil: Lays down a phat, driving bass line especially on Corinna, providing solid foundation throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals are solid with a humorous slip on the "dark sard" line; his singing shines on Corinna.
band_performance.Bruce: Keyboard work adds vibrant texture and dynamic interplay, particularly during the Hornsby‑Garcia sections and the space jam.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides steady, propulsive rhythms that lift the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Billy: Delivers crisp drum work; his contributions help drive the high‑energy medleys.
